content_en:
The angels (mala'ikah) occupy a unique position in the Islamic cosmology as beings of light, created before humanity, who exist in continuous proximity to Allah and serve as the primary agents of divine administration in the universe. Al-Ashqar's treatment of the angelic world draws comprehensively on Quranic verses and authentic hadith to present what Muslims are obligated to believe about these noble beings.

Angels were created from light (nur). This is established in the authentic hadith: 'Angels were created from light, jinn were created from a smokeless flame of fire, and Adam was created from what has been described to you.' Their creation from light is consistent with their nature: beings of pure spiritual energy, free from the biological drives and material limitations that characterize the human being. They have no need for food or drink, no sexual nature, no sleep as humans experience it, and no disobedience to Allah built into their nature.

The number of angels far exceeds human comprehension. The Prophet described the heavens as groaning under the weight of angels performing prostration — so densely populated are they that 'there is no space of four fingers in the heavens except that an angel is standing, bowing, or prostrating in worship of Allah.' The celestial realm is thus depicted as a universe of worship in which angels constitute the primary worshippers, preceding humanity in the praise of their Lord.

Angels possess forms and wings. The Quran describes them as 'messengers with wings, in pairs of two, three, and four' (Surah Fatir). The angel Jibril — the greatest and most frequently described — appeared to the Prophet in his original form with six hundred wings, filling the entire horizon. When angels appear in human form for their missions (as they did with Ibrahim and Lut), they take on the appearance of humans but retain their angelic nature.

Angels are fundamentally characterized by perfect obedience. The Quran says: 'They do not disobey Allah in what He commands them, and they do what they are commanded.' Unlike human beings who struggle with moral agency, the angels have no capacity for disobedience built into their nature. This is not because they are constrained against their will, but because their will is entirely in alignment with their Creator's — a condition that the Islamic tradition presents as the highest form of freedom, not the absence of it. Their entire existence is worship, and they neither tire of it nor fall short in it.
content_id:
Para malaikat (malaika) menempati posisi unik dalam kosmologi Islam sebagai makhluk cahaya, yang diciptakan sebelum manusia, yang selalu berada dalam kedekatan dengan Allah dan berfungsi sebagai agen utama administrasi ilahi di alam semesta. Kajian al-Asyqar tentang alam malaikat secara komprehensif berlandaskan ayat-ayat Al-Quran dan hadis sahih untuk menyajikan apa yang wajib diimani oleh umat Muslim tentang makhluk-makhluk mulia ini.

Malaikat diciptakan dari cahaya (nur). Hal ini ditetapkan dalam hadis sahih: Malaikat diciptakan dari cahaya, jin diciptakan dari nyala api yang tidak berasap, dan Adam diciptakan dari apa yang telah dijelaskan kepada kalian. Penciptaan mereka dari cahaya sesuai dengan hakikat mereka: makhluk dari energi spiritual murni, bebas dari dorongan biologis dan keterbatasan material yang menjadi ciri manusia. Mereka tidak membutuhkan makanan atau minuman, tidak memiliki sifat seksual, tidak tidur sebagaimana manusia mengalaminya, dan tidak memiliki kemampuan bermaksiat kepada Allah dalam fitrah mereka.

Jumlah malaikat jauh melampaui pemahaman manusia. Nabi menggambarkan langit-langit berderit di bawah beban malaikat yang sedang bersujud, begitu padatnya mereka sehingga tidak ada ruang selebar empat jari di langit kecuali ada malaikat yang berdiri, rukuk, atau bersujud dalam ibadah kepada Allah. Alam langit dengan demikian digambarkan sebagai alam semesta ibadah di mana malaikat merupakan para penyembah utama, mendahului manusia dalam memuji Tuhan mereka.

Malaikat memiliki bentuk dan sayap. Al-Quran menggambarkan mereka sebagai utusan-utusan bersayap, berpasangan dua, tiga, dan empat (Surah Fatir). Malaikat Jibril, yang terbesar dan paling sering digambarkan, menampakkan diri kepada Nabi dalam wujud aslinya dengan enam ratus sayap, memenuhi seluruh cakrawala. Ketika malaikat menampakkan diri dalam wujud manusia untuk misi mereka, seperti yang mereka lakukan kepada Ibrahim dan Lut, mereka mengambil penampilan manusia tetapi tetap mempertahankan hakikat malaikat mereka.

Malaikat secara fundamental dicirikan oleh ketaatan yang sempurna. Al-Quran berfirman: Mereka tidak mendurhakai Allah dalam apa yang Dia perintahkan kepada mereka, dan mereka mengerjakan apa yang diperintahkan. Berbeda dengan manusia yang berjuang dengan pilihan moral, malaikat tidak memiliki kemampuan untuk bermaksiat yang tertanam dalam hakikat mereka. Bukan karena mereka dipaksa melawan kehendak mereka, melainkan karena kehendak mereka sepenuhnya selaras dengan kehendak Pencipta mereka, suatu kondisi yang oleh tradisi Islam disajikan sebagai bentuk kebebasan tertinggi, bukan ketiadaannya. Seluruh keberadaan mereka adalah ibadah, dan mereka tidak pernah merasa lelah karenanya maupun kurang dalam menjalankannya.
content_ar: The الملائكة (mala'ikah) occupy a unique position in the Islamic cosmology as beings of light, created قبل humanity, who exist in continuous proximity to الله and serve as the الأساسي agents of divine administration in the universe. Al-Ashqar's treatment of the angelic الدنيا draws comprehensively on القرآني الآيات and authentic الحديث to present what المسلمون are obligated to believe about these noble beings. الملائكة were created from light (nur). This is established in the authentic الحديث: 'الملائكة were created from light, الجن were created from a smokeless flame of fire, and آدم was created from what has been described to you.' Their creation from light is consistent with their nature: beings of pure spiritual energy, free from the biological drives and material limitations that characterize the human being. They have no need for food or drink, no sexual nature, no sleep as humans experience it, and no disobedience to الله built into their nature. The number of الملائكة far exceeds human comprehension. النبي ﷺ described the heavens as groaning under the weight of الملائكة performing prostration — لذا densely populated are they that 'there is no space of four fingers in the heavens except that an angel is standing, bowing, or prostrating in worship of الله.' The celestial realm is وهكذا depicted as a universe of worship in which الملائكة constitute the الأساسي worshippers, preceding humanity in the praise of their Lord. الملائكة possess forms and wings. The القرآن الكريم describes them as 'الرسل with wings, in pairs of two, three, and four' (السورة Fatir). The angel جبريل ﷺ — the greatest and most frequently described — appeared to النبي ﷺ in his original form with six hundred wings, filling the entire horizon. عندما الملائكة appear in human form for their missions (as they did with إبراهيم ﷺ and Lut), they take on the appearance of humans but retain their angelic nature. الملائكة are بشكل أساسي characterized by perfect obedience. The القرآن الكريم says: 'They do not disobey الله in what He commands them, and they do what they are commanded.' Unlike human beings who struggle with moral agency, the الملائكة have no capacity for disobedience built into their nature. This is not لأن they are constrained against their will, but لأن their will is entirely in alignment with their Creator's — a condition that the Islamic التراث presents as the highest form of freedom, not the absence of it. Their entire existence is worship, and they neither tire of it nor fall short in it.
